# Skill Protocol (create-loop skill proper)

Covers the installable skill's design: the entrypoint (`SKILL.md`), the layered execution model, the locked vocabulary in `references/`, the run-artifact templates, and worked examples. This is the payload the installer copies verbatim into a host.

## Core Invariants

- `SKILL.md` MUST stay under 1000 lines (hard budget enforced by `tests/acceptance_tests.md`) — depth belongs in `references/` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:8, 48].
- The 15 node statuses and the 8 subgraph statuses are DISJOINT enums; never apply one set to the other [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:32-33, 49].
- Every `loop.plan` node carries all 21 fields; the 21st, `child_loops`, is REQUIRED on every node with empty sentinel `[]` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:41].
- A node reaches `completed` ONLY when its latest evidence-ledger entry has `verdict: pass`; otherwise `verification_failed` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:42].
- The skill MUST NOT write into `.agents/knowledge/` (the durable self-evolution store) — the transient/durable boundary is strict [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:54].
- Never change top-level `goal`/`true_intent`/`non_goals`/`deliverable_class` without user confirmation [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:52].

## Conventions

- Three-layer model: Layer 0 Charter interview (`templates/task_profile.yaml`, control profile only) → Layer 1 `loop.plan v0` (`design_invariant: true` governance nodes, no vendor names/paths/tests) → Layer 2 runtime subgraphs materialized inside `mapper`/`allow_subgraph` nodes [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:17-20].
- Source-of-truth order when editing the skill: `references/loop_plan_spec.md` + `state_model.md` (locked vocabulary) → `references/recursive_loops.md` + `subgraph_subloop_policy.md` → `schemas/*.json` → `scripts/checks/__init__.py` → `SKILL.md` reference map [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:24-29].
- Extend workflow (add gate/node/status): edit refs → update `schemas/*.json` → update `scripts/` → add fixture in `tests/failure_mode_tests.md` → re-run acceptance gate [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:45].
- Directory naming: underscore-prefixed dirs (`_loops/`, `_archive/`) are control structure; plain names (`artifacts/`, `loop.plan.yaml`) are work content [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:43].
- Per-loop directory layout is isomorphic at every depth — root, child, grandchild all carry the same file set [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:44].
- Loop IDs are immutable: top-level `L<seq>` (3-digit), child `<parent>.<seq>` (2-digit), dir `L<seq>-<slug>` under `.agents/loops/`, children under `_loops/` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:38].

## Common Mistakes

- Editing `loop.plan.yaml` and `checkpoint.yaml` independently — both must agree on `plan_id`, `plan_version`, and every `node_id` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:50].
- Asking the user first on a branch/unknown/blocker — the autonomy-first principle says spawn a subgraph and gather evidence; escalate ONLY at a genuine §3 boundary (goal, scope, irreversible/external side effect, cost, legal, value, authorization) [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:22, 51].
- Deleting a retired node — retired nodes must be tombstoned with `retirement{type,reason}`; inactive evidence must never keep `verdict: pass` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:53].
- Editing `SKILL.md` and its `references/` vocabulary independently — the references are source of truth; SKILL.md must agree [source: AGENTS.md:54].

## Verified Facts

- `SKILL.md` is 699 lines, under the 1000-line budget [source: .agents/knowledge/reference/.project-scan.txt, Largest files; skills/create-loop/SKILL.md].
- `references/` holds 18 docs; `templates/` holds 19 fill-in artifacts; `examples/` holds 3 worked loops (one a recursive child-loop tree) [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:9-14; find counts 2026-07-03].
- 8 node kinds: milestone, gate, mapper, branch, fanout, join, approval, compensation [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:34].
- 8 gate kinds: automated_check, test, llm_judge, self_consistency, evaluator_optimizer, step_verifier, human_approval, artifact_exists [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:35].
- 4-rung ordered escalation ladder: local_retry → local_patch → replan → escalate [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:36].
- 3 execution tiers by governance need (not size): `action` → `subgraph` → `subloop` [source: skills/create-loop/AGENTS.md:37].

## Working Understanding

- The skill is filesystem-based with no daemon/DB; "degraded mode" (no subagents/hooks) is the same contract with fewer conveniences, not a second implementation [source: AGENTS.md:46, skills/create-loop/README.md §4 Notes].
- Optional self-evolution integration exists: verified reusable findings can be promoted to a `.agents/knowledge/` store via the `[LOOP]` channel marker, but only if that store already exists [source: skills/create-loop/README.md §3 Optional self-evolution integration].
